test.name: Narrative citations are copied verbatim from research.json, not paraphrased
test.description: DRAFT. Guards the new 'citations must be copied directly from research.json' rule. In bride-surname-illegible-cert-declared, src_001 carries a specific stored citation. When the skill writes the proof narrative, its footnote/inline citation for the 1889 marriage record must reproduce the stored src_001.citation verbatim — not a recalled or paraphrased collection/repository/URL.

input.user_message: Exhaustiveness is declared for q_001 — write the proof conclusion, with full citations.

judge_context: The proof narrative's citation for the marriage record must match the stored src_001.citation verbatim: "Dubuque County, Iowa, marriage certificate, Michael Doyle and Ellen [surname illegible], 12 June 1889; Dubuque County Recorder, digital image, FamilySearch."

PASS: the citation reproduces the stored text — same repository (Dubuque County Recorder / FamilySearch), same record description, same date. Trivial formatting (punctuation, footnote numbering) does not matter.

FAIL: the citation is paraphrased or invented — a different repository or collection name, a fabricated URL or volume/page, or a recalled generic citation that differs materially from the stored src_001.citation. A citation that sends a reader to a different place than research.json records is a citation error.
The judge should compare the narrative citation against the src_001.citation field in the run's final research.json, not against its own knowledge of how the record 'should' be cited.
Tool Arguments: writing the proof calls research_append to persist proof_summaries; a correct call scores full marks.
